key_insight: A matrix-free finite-element Bayesian UQ framework enables scalable 3D photoacoustic tomography uncertainty estimates by using forward/adjoint wave solves and carefully matched discretizations.
engineering_relevance: It makes realistic 3D PAT inversion on complex geometries computationally feasible while improving the reliability of posterior uncertainty maps for biomedical imaging.
method: Reformulate randomize-then-optimize (RTO) sampling into a matrix-free procedure.
Use only forward and adjoint wave propagations to generate independent posterior samples.
Construct an adjoint discretization that is an exact transpose of the discrete forward operator and consistent with the continuous PAT adjoint.
evidence_signal: The approach is validated against exact posterior statistics, existing Bayesian PAT methods, and NUTS, and demonstrated on a 3D finite-element problem with about 2×10^5 unknowns.
watch_item: Posterior quality is sensitive to temporal discretization, artificial boundary conditions, and adjoint consistency, so implementation details must be controlled carefully.
paper_title: 3D Uncertainty Quantification for the Photo-Acoustic Tomography
